text.title: Task File Template: Rules and Fill Order
text.summary: Rules for correctly creating TASK_NNN.md files: section responsibilities (creator vs executor), token budget enforcement, INVEST validation, lifecycle states, and anti-patterns.

rule.statement: Task creator fills SDD References, Dependency Tree, Requirements Coverage, Objective, Dependencies, AC, Technical Approach, Files, and Estimated Tokens. Executor fills Implementation and Summary. Never pre-fill executor sections.
rule.rationale: Pre-filled Implementation sections give the executor incorrect expectations and reduce quality — the executor follows the pre-fill rather than the actual codebase.

list.item: Creator fills: SDD References, Task Dependency Tree, Requirements Coverage, Objective, Dependencies, Acceptance Criteria, Technical Approach, Files, Estimated Tokens
Executor fills: Implementation (Changes Made, Tests Added), Summary (Completed, Issues, Lessons)

rule.statement: Every task must have an Estimated Tokens breakdown, and the total must be under 100k tokens.
rule.rationale: Tasks exceeding 100k tokens cause executor agents to run out of context mid-implementation, producing partial or inconsistent code.

example.note: Token budget breakdown for a normal-complexity task
example.code: SDD Documents (constitution + spec + design): ~15k Task Dependency Tree (done task summaries): ~10k Research (read existing code patterns): ~25k Implementation (actual coding): ~40k Testing (writing tests): ~10k TOTAL: ~100k

list.item: Simple task: <30k tokens — single file, clear pattern
Normal task: 30-60k tokens — multiple files, some decisions
Complex task: 60-100k tokens — architecture decisions, research needed

rule.statement: Paste the full text of each FR-X and AD-X inline into the task file. Do not write "See spec.md" — the executor must not re-read the full SDD at runtime.
rule.rationale: Each re-read of the full spec adds 10-30k tokens of context cost and risks the executor misreading a section out of context.

rule.statement: The Objective must be a single, measurable, agent-executable goal in 2-3 sentences. If the title contains "and", split the task.
rule.rationale: Tasks with compound objectives are executed partially — agents complete the first part and mark done without attempting the second.

example.code: Create User model with email validation and password hashing and add login endpoint.
Create User model with email field (Zod RFC 5322 validation) and password_hash field (bcrypt wrapper). Expose via UserRepository interface.

rule.statement: Task lifecycle is directory-based: todo/ → in-progress/ → done/. The Status field in the task header must match the directory. Never place a task in the wrong directory.
rule.rationale: Executor agents check the directory, not the status field. A task in todo/ is picked up regardless of its header status.

antipattern.description: Vague acceptance criteria: "system works correctly" or "user experience is smooth".
antipattern.reason: Executor agents cannot verify vague criteria. Every AC must have an observable, code-verifiable outcome.

antipattern.description: Given-When-Then format used for background jobs (no "when" actor).
antipattern.reason: Use "Given / Triggered by / Then" variant for jobs with no user actor.

antipattern.description: Estimated Tokens for test files set to <10k.
antipattern.reason: Test files for Django viewsets or React components consistently run 30-40k tokens. Underestimating causes executor to run out of context before tests are complete.

text.reference: Complete TASK_NNN.md template with all required sections
Moves task file between lifecycle states with section header validation
